summary: Large partial decompilation of src/melee/db/db_2253.c focused on Melee debug/dev functionality. The PR replaced many placeholder `/// #...` stubs with C implementations, added or corrected prototypes across headers, introduced supporting declarations/static data in db_2253.static.h, and made a Dolphin OSContext layout correction needed by FPU save/load assembly. decomp.dev reported +3736 matched bytes and +0.10% matched code, with many db_2253 functions reaching 100% while later functions remained partial.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; grew from stubs into many C implementations for debug input handling, item/debug toggles, DevText display, CARD/PAD polling, and debug initialization.
Supporting static/global declarations and struct definitions for db_2253.c; largest header-side support change.
Public prototypes changed from broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` placeholders to typed signatures for many newly decompiled functions.
Corrected OSContext FPU status area by splitting the old f64 fpscr field into `u32 fpscr_pad` at 0x190 and `u32 fpscr` at 0x194.
Updated FPU save/load assembly member references from `fpuctx->fpscr` to `fpuctx->fpscr_pad` to match the corrected struct layout.
Typed camera prototypes used by db_2253.c, replacing several unknown signatures.
Added allocation-related declarations needed by the decompilation.
Adjusted particle declarations used by the debug code.
Adjusted game-mode prototypes used by db_2253.c.
Adjusted interface/all headers for typed dependencies.
Adjusted item type declarations used by debug item manipulation.
Adjusted player bonus declarations, likely for debug name/common-data references.
Adjusted library shadow declarations used by this debug module or dependent prototypes.

what_changed: Implemented `db_80225374`, which waits for stable PAD status, stores detected button state in `db_804D6B30`, and waits for `CARDProbeEx` on slot 0.

Implemented `db_802254B8`, initializing debug input state when `g_debugLevel >= 3`, loading `DbCo.dat` via `lbArchive_80016C64`, wiring `bonus_names`, `motionstate_names`, and `submotion_names`, and calling a series of db setup functions.
Added small accessors `fn_8022558C`, `fn_802255A4`, and `fn_802255BC` returning fields from `db_8049FA00`.
Implemented reporting helpers such as `db_802255D4` for GObj link counts and `db_802256CC` for stack/thread info using `_stack_addr`, `_stack_end`, and `OSReport`.
Implemented debug pad-state processing in `db_80225754`, including per-player button/repeat filtering, edge-state computation, and dispatch to many debug subsystem handlers.
Implemented item/debug state setup and toggles around `db_8049FAA0`, including bitfield accesses such as `db_8049FAA0.x20.b0`, `.b1`, and `.b2`.
Implemented item iteration helpers such as `fn_80225B48`, `fn_80225B9C`, `fn_80225BF0`, `fn_80225C44`, `fn_80225C8C`, and `fn_80225CD4`, manipulating item flags on `HSD_GObj_Entities->items`.
Implemented item owner/debug-byte handlers `db_80225D64`, `fn_80225D7C`, `db_80225DD8`, and `fn_80225E6C` using `GET_ITEM`, item owner checks, and `db_804D6B3C` cycling.
Implemented partial item spawn/debug menu routines around `fn_80225F20`, `fn_802260D4`, `fn_802261BC`, `fn_802262E0`, and `fn_802264C4`; some remained partial per decomp.dev percentages.
Changed many `db_2253.h` prototypes from `UNK_RET ... (UNK_PARAMS)` to typed signatures such as `void db_802254B8(void)`, `int fn_8022558C(int)`, and `void fn_80225E6C(Fighter_GObj* owner)`.
Updated camera prototypes used by the module, including `void Camera_8002FEEC(int)`, `int Camera_80030154(void)`, `int Camera_80030178(void)`, and `void Camera_80030AA4(int)`.
Corrected `OSContext` layout so the FPU status save area has a 32-bit pad and 32-bit fpscr member instead of a single f64, and updated assembly references accordingly.
smart_moves: Used typed prototypes and includes instead of leaving broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, making the new C compile against real cross-module interfaces and improving future callsite matching.

Introduced tiny static inline helpers `db_get_pad_button` and `db_get_pad_repeat` for `HSD_PadMasterStatus[i & 0xFF]`; this keeps repeated indexed access readable while preserving the exact masked indexing pattern visible in the diff.
Modeled `db_8049FAA0.x20` as bitfields (`b0`, `b1`, `b2`) instead of separate pseudo-fields like `lbl_8049FAA0.x20_b0`, allowing flag access to match item/debug state code more naturally.

Used local anonymous structs where only a small loaded data layout was needed, such as the `commonData` struct with `bonus_names`, `motionstate_names`, and `submotion_names`, avoiding overcommitting global type names from weak evidence.
Recorded an explicit matching workaround in `db_80225DD8`: calling `db_80225D64(item, owner)` was commented out with `// stack too big`, and its body was inlined to preserve the stack shape.
Left an in-code TODO for a known incorrect data-section-dependent lookup in `fn_802261BC`, which prevents future readers from treating a partial matching workaround as final understanding.
Fixed an underlying SDK struct layout issue (`fpscr_pad` vs `fpscr`) rather than papering over assembly member references, which benefits all users of OSContext.
decomp_lessons: Partial PRs can still merge substantial value when they convert a long unknown file into many individually matched functions; here decomp.dev showed many early db_2253 functions at 100% even though later routines remained partial.

When decompiling debug/menu code, expect broad dependencies across PAD/CARD/VI, DevText, fighter, item, camera, and game-mode headers; adding precise prototypes in those headers is part of the matching work.
Be careful with stack-sensitive helper extraction. A semantically correct helper call can change stack size enough to break matching, as documented by the `// stack too big` comment in `db_80225DD8`.

If data sections are not yet correctly modeled, keep TODOs at lookup sites rather than inventing final arrays or names. The PR explicitly marks one item-name path as wrong until the data section is good.

Correct struct layout at the source when assembly member references expose wrong offsets. The OSContext change shows that a named padding field can be necessary even if the old aggregate type looked equivalent in size.

Replacing address-labeled globals like `lbl_8049FAA0` with module-prefixed names and structured fields can make downstream code much more understandable, but field names remained offset-style (`x0`, `x10`, etc.) where semantics were not established.
Header cleanup is a matching multiplier: many newly decompiled functions required changing external declarations from unknown varargs-like placeholders to specific return and argument types.
naming_conventions: Newly decompiled functions preserve project address-based names such as `db_80225374` for known module-prefixed symbols and `fn_80225A00` for unnamed functions.
Global/static symbols use address-based module names such as `db_8049FA00`, `db_8049FAA0`, `db_804D6B30`, and `db_804D6B3C` rather than generic `lbl_` names when enough module ownership is known.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, etc.) when semantic names are not proven.
Bitfields are represented with nested names such as `db_8049FAA0.x20.b0`, `.b1`, and `.b2` instead of flattened pseudo-fields.
Header declarations retain address comments like `/* 2254B8 */ void db_802254B8(void);`, matching existing doldecomp style.
External functions continue to use subsystem prefixes and address names, for example `Camera_80030154`, `ftLib_800860E8`, `Item_80266F3C`, and `lbArchive_80016C64`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed `OSContext.c` inline assembly references from `fpuctx->fpscr` to `fpuctx->fpscr_pad` after splitting the struct fields, aligning `lfd`/`stfd` with the intended doubleword at offset 0x190.
Used explicit stack dummy arrays such as `int stack[2]` and `int stack[4]` in several functions, indicating stack-shape matching pressure.
Inlined a small helper body in `db_80225DD8` instead of calling `db_80225D64` because the call caused the stack to be too large.
Used masked array indexing patterns like `HSD_PadMasterStatus[i & 0xFF]`, likely to preserve generated code behavior.

Kept some routines partial rather than forcing inaccurate matches; decomp.dev reported functions such as `fn_80225A54`, `fn_80225F20`, `fn_802260D4`, `fn_802261BC`, `fn_802262E0`, and others below 100%.
Included an infinite loop fallback in one debug item-name selection path, matching observed control flow for out-of-range cases in the diff excerpt.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased to 26.76% (+0.10%, +3736 bytes) for GALE01.
Identified remaining partial matches such as `fn_80225A54` at 54.35%, `fn_80225F20` at 22.02%, `fn_802260D4` at 72.41%, `fn_802261BC` at 93.15%, `fn_802262E0` at 61.98%, and several later functions.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The diff contains `// db_80225D64(item, owner); // stack too big`, documenting a matching-driven inlining decision.
The diff contains `// TODO: this is wrong. return when the data section is good`, documenting a known data-section limitation.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `src/melee/db/db_2253.c` and functions still partial after PR 1517, especially `fn_80225A54`, `fn_80225F20`, `fn_802260D4`, `fn_802261BC`, and `fn_802262E0`.
Search for PRs that complete or rename `db_8049FAA0` and `db_8049FA00` fields to semantic names.
Search for PRs fixing the TODO about item-name data section lookup in `fn_802261BC`.
Search for downstream effects of the `OSContext` `fpscr_pad` layout change in other Dolphin OS context code.
Search for other PRs that use matching-driven inlining because a helper call made the stack too big.
